creator: freebsd-pr@fabparis.no-ip.org
text:
Hello,
        I want to use the e-sata external port to hotplug a sata hard disk drive and make backups.
        As we can see in dmesg output hotplug works fine and camcontrol see the HDD.
 	fdisk -BI /dev/ada4 and bsdlabel -w /dev/ada4 works fine and return "0".
        Error occurs when running newfs -U /dev/ada4a, here is output :
srvbart# newfs -U /dev/ada4a
/dev/ada4a: 1907729.1MB (3907029152 sectors) block size 16384, fragment size 2048
        using 10384 cylinder groups of 183.72MB, 11758 blks, 23552 inodes.
        with soft updates
super-block backups (for fsck -b #) at:
 160, 376416, 752672, 1128928, 1505184, 1881440, 2257696, 2633952, 3010208, 3386464, 3762720, 4138976, 4515232, 4891488, 5267744, 5644000, 6020256, 6396512, 6772768, 7149024, 7525280, 7901536, 8277792, 8654048, 9030304, 9406560,
 9782816, 10159072, 10535328, 10911584, 11287840, 11664096, 12040352, 12416608, 12792864, 13169120, 13545376, 13921632, 14297888, 14674144, 15050400, 15426656, 15802912, 16179168, 16555424, 16931680, 17307936, 17684192, 18060448,
 18436704, 18812960, 19189216, 19565472, 19941728, 20317984, 20694240, 21070496, 21446752, 21823008, 22199264, 22575520, 22951776, 23328032, 23704288, 24080544, 24456800, 24833056, 25209312, 25585568, 25961824, 26338080, 26714336,
 27090592, 27466848, 27843104, 28219360, 28595616, 28971872, 29348128, 29724384, 30100640, 30476896, 30853152, 31229408, 31605664, 31981920,newfs: wtfs: 65536 bytes at sector 32358176: Input/output error

	When re-running the same command it fails in the same way but not at the same timei, in fact more faster.
	
	The PR amd64/135040 tels problem abount having more than 4G memory could have problem with some device drivers.
	I have installed freebsd i386 version and have got the same problem.

	To check if the hard drive is in "fault", I have installed debian amd64 on this external drive and it works fine out of the box.
	This enclosure use Jmicron JM20329 but it seems only for sata to usb mode and not used in esata mode.

	Four other hard drive "WDC WD20EARS-00MVWB0 51.0AB51" are connected to 4 internal sata port which are managed by the same controler (intel H55) as the externel one,
	I use thoses without any problem.

How-To-Repeat: 	Use ahci kernel module with GENERIC kernel, hot plug external device on an H55 esata port.
	Create a whole disk slice and make one partition with bsdlabel.
	Then run newfs for this new partition with or without -U flag.
